mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above the historical trend signals robust aggregate demand, raising corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month. When GDPC1 rises above its 4-quarter moving average by more than 1%, the mechanism is active.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 increases above the 4-quarter moving average by more than 1% and the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 55% of such events over a 3-year walk-forward window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate above 7% constrains consumer spending and weakens corporate profit outlook, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns. The signal is triggered when UNRATE exceeds 7% and remains above that threshold for at least two consecutive months.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 7% for two consecutive months but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation rises while the federal funds rate remains at the zero lower bound, real interest rates fall further negative, compressing risk premiums and inflating equity valuations. This interaction of rising CPI and low FEDFUNDS amplifies positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: If the joint signal of CPIAUCSL rising month-over-month and FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% is followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 4-year walk-forward, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-Great Recession recovery phase, robust nonfarm payroll gains signal a self-reinforcing cycle of income growth and consumer spending, which directly supports equity prices. The mechanism is strongest when monthly PAYEMS increases exceed 200,000 and the recovery is less than 5 years old.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS monthly increases exceed 200,000 during the 2009-2014 recovery period but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 30% of instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production index indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength. This supports corporate profits and drives positive short-term equity returns as the market prices near-term activity improvements.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increased over the latest 3 months and the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative in more than half of such instances over a 2-year walk-forward, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 500,000 units annualized indicate chronic weakness in residential investment, a leading demand component. Persistently low HOUST forecasts a sluggish economic backdrop that depresses equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 500 (thousands of units annualized) for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If HOUST remains below 500 for two consecutive months but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of such episodes over a 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and decline in unemployment reflects strong consumption fundamentals. When RSAFS rises month-over-month and UNRATE falls, consumer-driven GDP momentum becomes a powerful positive signal for equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases and UNRATE decreases in the same month but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such months over a 4-year walk-forward, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, a sustained rise in the CPI inflation rate beyond 2% erodes real incomes and forces eventual monetary tightening expectations. This anticipated tightening depresses equity valuations, making the mechanism negatively directional in the zero-lower-bound regime.
regime_hint: zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%) with creeping inflation
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises above 2% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of such episodes over a 3-year walk-forward, reject the hypothesis.
